title: Schezwan Cauliflower Fried Rice
description: A low-carb cauliflower fried rice loaded with vegetables, tofu, extra garlic, eggs, and spicy Schezwan sauce.

ingredients.note: grated or pulsed into rice-sized pieces
optional; omit for a vegan version

steps: Pulse the cauliflower in a food processor until it resembles rice. Do not over-process.
Heat the oil in a large wok over high heat. Add the garlic and ginger and stir-fry for about a minute until fragrant.
Add the white parts of the green onions, carrots, green beans, mushrooms, and corn. Cook for 3–4 minutes until just tender.
Push the vegetables to one side. Scramble the eggs, if using, then mix them into the vegetables.
Add the crumbled tofu and cook until lightly browned.
Stir in the cauliflower rice and cook over high heat for 4–5 minutes, allowing excess moisture to evaporate.
Mix together the Schezwan sauce, soy sauce, and rice vinegar. Pour over the cauliflower rice and toss well.
Season with black pepper and salt if needed. Finish with sesame oil and the green parts of the onions.
Serve immediately. Garnish with extra green onions and pair with crispy tofu, spring rolls, or a side of soy sauce.
notes: High heat is key to keeping the cauliflower from becoming soggy.
Extra garlic gives the dish much of its flavor—don't reduce it unless preferred.
Add chili flakes or sliced bird's-eye chilies for additional heat.
For more protein, increase the tofu to 250 g or add edamame.
